DCTH.body:
$DCTH has announced that the first patient has been dosed at the European Institute of Oncology (IEO) in Milan, Italy, in its global Phase 2 clinical trial evaluating HEPZATO in combination with standard of care treatment for liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer (mBC). 
 
https://biotuesdays.com/2026/07/09/first-patient-dosed-in-delcaths-global-trial-of-hepzato-in-mbc/

$DCTH 
 
Another interesting setup showing why price action matters. 
 
Different sectors, different stories, but the same trading principle keeps appearing: respect the levels, watch the structure, and let the chart confirm the move. 
 
$DCTH has been following the technical setup closely, with buyers stepping in at key areas and momentum continuing to develop. 
 
This is why I focus on process over predictions. Good setups can appear anywhere when you understand trend, support, and momentum. 
 
The market always creates opportunities. The goal is being ready when they show up.

$DCTH first patient dosed in Phase 2 HEPZATO trial for liver-dominant HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer. This is Delcath’s shot to show HEPZATO is a platform, not a single-indication play. Watching data and timelines closely. 
#DCTH #Biotech #Oncology #HEPZATO 
https://prismmarketview.com/delcath-systems-starts-phase-2-hepzato-trial-in-liver-dominant-metastatic-breast-cancer/
$DCTH This is exactly how my mother died. Breast cancer followed by liver cancer. Good luck to Delcath.
$DCTH Alex - Do you know if a drug company pays the trial sites for participating?
i sold my adobe thankfully. trading is always stressful. back to the ole barn with my long term holds in $STXS $ABEO $DCTH $MIST
$DCTH quiet on the message board, good price, that is about it

$DCTH It was just 2 weeks ago Dcth was fighting to finally close over 12 as everyday it went over 12 and they took it back down until finally closing over 12. 
Now less than 2 weeks later after several tries over 13 we close over 13 and we are closing in on 52 week high from August. 
Nice to see the way this is progressing and nice way to go into the long weekend.
$DCTH Last day of 2 Qtr......Let's hope and pray Revs are north of $25MM.

$DCTH  📚 Why $DCTH is a high-conviction play right now:

Delcath is commercializing HEPZATO KIT, a breakthrough liver-directed chemotherapy system for unresectable hepatic metastases (ocular melanoma and beyond). FY2026 revenue guidance is at least $100M with strong procedure growth and expanding adoption. Recent clinical data and ASCO updates continue to build the case.

Big Name Institutional Buyers:
Rosalind Advisors, BlackRock, Vanguard, Bank of America, and other institutions own over 50% of the company with ongoing accumulation.

LXEO.body:
$LXEO Happy with what we're seeing so far.  Looking forward to a nice close to the week as well. 
 
Follow/Subscribe for more suggestions and perspective.  Would love to hear yours too.  That's how we find these gems and learn. 
 
Let's make some (more) money.  Cheers!
$LRMR I think it's gonna be hard to avoid a black box warning regarding safety profile. 1/4 patients is incredibly relevant. Although earlier stage I think the future is gene therapy. $LXEO

$LXEO Announces Publication in JAMA Cardiology of Phase I/II Data for LX2006 in Friedreich Ataxia

- Published findings in JAMA Cardiology demonstrate LX2006 generally well tolerated, with early signs of efficacy in Phase I/II studies

- SUNRISE-FA 2 pivotal study for LX2006 on track to initiate in Q2 2026 with topline data expected in 2H 2027

$LRMR  
 
LXEO spikes today. 
 
FA has a very small market size, but it seems there are more and more companies offer drugs in FA treatment. 
 
$LXEO   and $DSGN and $BIIB  are the 3 companies in addition to LRMR.
$LXEO issued a press release detailing regulatory updates for its LX2006 gene therapy targeting Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy.

JPMorgan🏁 $LRMR and said: We are initiating coverage of Larimar Therapeutics (LRMR) with an Overweight rating and a Dec 2027 price target of $9. 
$PTCT SLDB BIIB DSGN $LXEO 
Larimar's lead asset is nomlabofusp (nomla; CTI-1601), a subcutaneously administered, recombinant fusion protein designed to deliver frataxin (FXN) to the mitochondria of patients with Friedreich's ataxia (FA), a rare neurodegenerative disorder. 
 
In the near-term, in 2Q26, we expect to see topline results from the ongoing open-label (OL) study as well as feedback from the company's type B meeting with the FDA to align on the overall BLA package readiness. 
 
The company expects a rolling BLA submission for accelerated approval (AA) in June 2026, pending FDA feedback, using skin FXN levels as a surrogate endpoint, with the final submission including the CMC module expected in 2H26; if approved, nomla would be the first FXN replacement therapy for FA, with a potential U.S. approval and launch in 1H27.
$LXEO  that response was stronger than most would have guessed.

NVO.body:
$NVO inks a long-acting GLP-1 implant tech deal with $VANI. 
 
Interesting setup for both names: 
$NVO keeps extending its obesity/diabetes toolkit 
$VANI gets validation on its implant platform 
 
Not a hype print, but this kind of pairing can keep sentiment constructive around GLP-1 delivery tech.

$NVO Novo Nordisk A/S (NYSE: NVO) isn't simply selling diabetes and obesity treatments—it has reshaped one of healthcare's fastest-growing markets. Find out why NVO stock remains a favorite among investors seeking exposure to GLP-1 drugs, peptide therapies, and long-term biotech growth. https://biotechhealthx.com/biotech-news/could-novo-nordisk-nvo-be-a-smart-long-term-investment-in-healthcare/

Strong early demand for $NVO’s oral Wegovy after the UK launch. 
 
Reports say pharmacies have already taken tens of thousands of orders since Monday. The tablet version should widen the addressable market for people who want a needle-free option, and that’s the key setup here.

 $NVO and $LLY are absolutely dominating the oral GLP-1 land grab. Next week is going to see an explosive move as institutional money rotates heavily into these pharma giants. Want to see the full, uncensored charts and my next price targets? Head over to my profile now!

Chasers are feeling smart after $NVO ripped 19% in a month. Bagholders are wondering why Wall St still won’t bless the move. 
 
Oral Wegovy scripts look strong, but sentiment is still ice cold: 12 Buys, 22 Holds, 3 Sells out of 37 analysts per Bloomberg. 
 
That’s not euphoria. That’s a stock climbing while the Street keeps one foot on the brake. Breakout fuel or trap door setup — no soft landing here.
